assertions.title: Interop-heavy programs emit deterministic, formatter-stable, buildable Go

steps.action: Compile the tracked fixture once with DUNE_BUILD_DIR=.../dune-build/run1
steps.expected: rgoc exits 0 and writes the first generated Go file under the evidence directory
steps.observed: `nix develop ... -c dune exec rgoc -- interop-crud.rg -o interop-crud-run1.go` exited 0 and wrote `crud-validation/crud-validation-determinism-cli/interop-crud-run1.go`; stdout contained only the Nix shell banner and stderr contained repeatable parser warnings from dune/menhir setup.

steps.action: Compile the same fixture again with DUNE_BUILD_DIR=.../dune-build/run2
steps.expected: Second rgoc run exits 0 and writes a second generated Go file under the evidence directory
steps.observed: `nix develop ... -c dune exec rgoc -- interop-crud.rg -o interop-crud-run2.go` exited 0 and wrote `crud-validation/crud-validation-determinism-cli/interop-crud-run2.go`; stdout/stderr shape matched the first run.

steps.action: Compare the two generated Go outputs byte-for-byte
steps.expected: Outputs are identical with stable helper naming/import ordering
steps.observed: `cmp -s` succeeded and `sha256sum` reported the same digest for both files: `6dc6bc2434a4ca82ef4236b209ff837b276e2ff6f681994c085d2924659b3f4a`.

steps.action: Run `nix develop -c gofmt -d` on the first generated Go file
steps.observed: After filtering the fixed 5-line Nix shell banner from stdout, the assertion-focused diff log was empty (`logs/gofmt-diff.cleaned.log` contains `empty`).

steps.action: Run `nix develop -c go build` on the first generated Go file
steps.expected: Go compiler accepts the emitted file and produces a binary
steps.observed: `go build` exited 0, produced `crud-validation/crud-validation-determinism-cli/interop-crud-run1.bin`, and wrote no stderr output.

steps.action: Run `nix develop -c go vet` on the first generated Go file
steps.observed: `go vet` exited 0 with an empty stderr log.

steps.action: Inspect the generated Go to confirm it is the CRUD interop fixture
steps.expected: Generated Go imports `net/http` and lowers CRUD handler/server operations from the fixture
steps.observed: The generated file imports `net/http`; lowers `r.method` to `r.Method`, `r.form_value("name")` to `r.FormValue("name")`, `w.write_header`/`w.write` to `w.WriteHeader`/`w.Write`, registers `/items` with `mux.HandleFunc("/items", handle_items)`, registers `/health` with an inline func literal, and starts the server with `http.ListenAndServe("127.0.0.1:3111", mux)`.

evidence.formatter: `gofmt -d` produced no diff after removing the fixed Nix shell banner from stdout.

frictions.description: `nix develop -c ...` prepended a fixed 5-line dev-shell banner to stdout, so the raw `gofmt -d` log was noisy even though the formatter produced no actual diff.
frictions.resolution: Captured `crud-validation/crud-validation-determinism-cli/logs/gofmt-diff.cleaned.log` by stripping the banner and verified the remaining content was empty.

summary: VAL-CROSS-003 passed. The tracked `interop-crud.rg` fixture compiled twice with isolated DUNE_BUILD_DIR values to byte-identical Go outputs, the emitted Go stayed formatter-stable, and downstream `go build`/`go vet` both exited 0. Generated Go inspection confirmed the expected `net/http` CRUD handler/server lowering.
